steps.title: Apply the triangle angle sum property
steps.content:
The sum of all angles in any triangle equals $180°$. Set up the equation:
$$A + B + C = 180°$$
Substitute the given values $B = 52°$ and $C = 17°$:
$$A + 52° + 17° = 180°$$

steps.title: Solve for angle A
steps.content:
Combine the known angles on the left side:
$$A + 69° = 180°$$
Subtract $69°$ from both sides:
$$A = 180° - 69° = 111°$$
That matches choice D — D is correct.
